title: QUIC CRT resumption-ticket ownership and early-data gate work item
completion_notes: This is intentionally a prerequisite slice. It records opaque ticket ownership and an explicit closed gate only; PSK derivation and any 0-RTT packet path stay deferred.
summary: Own opaque resumption-ticket state on the managed client/runtime path and keep early data gated closed until that state exists.
verification_plan: When implemented, run focused requirement-home tests for ticket capture, retention, replay wiring, and negative 0-RTT admission; run the current CRT/API/INT guards and the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep; run repo-local SpecTrace validation and render checks after the canonical JSON updates.
planned_changes:
- Add an internal opaque resumption-ticket carrier to the client connection host/runtime boundary.
- Capture ticket material from the TLS bridge when available and retain it for later connection replay.
- Thread the owned state through the existing connection bootstrap path without altering transfer, retry, or key-update behavior.
- Add an explicit early-data admission gate that keeps 0-RTT closed until the owned state exists.
- Add focused requirement-home tests for absence of ticket state, capture/retention, replay wiring, and negative 0-RTT admission once the runtime seam exists.
- Keep PSK derivation, 0-RTT packet processing, anti-replay guarantees, and public API widening out of scope.
